The next court discipline I want to talk about is how to write a lead-generating PDF. This is going to be a blast for you. Anybody who's ever wanted to write a book, you're going to have a little mini-feeling about writing a book because you get to write a beginning and an end of, say, a magazine-style article or an interview. Maybe you'll even have your name on it if your client wants your name on it. But the lead-generating PDF is a very powerful tool to bring attention to your client, establish trust, and really offer free value to the end user, to the customer. So there's three things I want you to remember when you're writing this lead-generating PDF. The first is it must provide enormous value for customers. Think about solving a problem. That's going to be the best lead-generating PDF, how to get your dog to stop barking when somebody rings the doorbell. That's a lead-generating PDF that owners of puppies might want to download, which is great because you would have their email address or your client would have their email address and they could then on-ramp them. You want to think about a problem. The human brain works like a Rolodex. When you hand somebody your business card, they don't put you in their Rolodex under your business name alphabetically. They actually put you in the Rolodex of their brain. They file you under a problem and you're going to help resolve a toothache or a dog biting or a weight problem or something like this. When you identify a problem and you identify a resolution to that problem in a lead-generating PDF, it causes customers to remember that brand and remember that company. So when you're thinking about lead-generating PDFs, solve a problem. That is going to be the most valuable thing that you can do for a potential customer. The second is you want to establish your client as the authority in their area of expertise. You want to sort of state claim to their territory. You want to help them be known for something. So these are the people who are experts at online banking or these are the people who are experts at in-home catering. You want to establish them so that when people think about them, they think about your client as the expert at this. And so make it very clear what it is they're an expert on. If you sell garden material, you want your clients to become known as the experts in your area on outdoor gardening. And so you want that PDF to establish territory, state claim to a territory. We are the experts at this. The other thing you want to do is you want to create reciprocity on behalf of your clients. When potential customers get that much value for free, they feel indebted to your client to do business with them. It's not a conscious feeling, but they feel this sense of, hey, you gave me something. I really want to give you something back. And so when it's time for them to place an order or they sense that problem and they need that problem resolved, they're much more likely to go and do business with your client. So there are a number of kinds of PDFs that you can create. And I just want to go through a few of them. The first is the magazine article. So think about small amounts of text, lots of images. And even though you're the copywriter, you want to think about the way the images will flow with the text. You're probably going to be tempted to write too much. I would encourage you to shorten that text down and let the images do a lot of speaking. I know you're not the designer, but you want to give the designer some cues. You want to be able to put parenthetically in the text. You might have a picture that emphasizes this point here. Give the designer some tips on how to wrap your text around something that looks like a magazine article. Have a great title. Keep it short. Think about callouts. Those are those blocks of text, maybe one sentence that you want to call out and highlight, maybe a quote. Think about a magazine article style of PDF. The next would be the interview. People love reading celebrity or expert interviews. And so you might even interview the leader of the company or one of the researchers at the company or one of the scientists if you're working for a company that has scientists and get really interesting information out of them and then build that as an interview with an expert on such and such topic. Also the checklist. The checklist is a fascinating lead generating PDF. We helped one company create a healthy business checklist and the checklist walked through five potential problems that a company might have. And of course those five potential problems that a company would have also correlated with the revenue streams of the other company. So any company that self-diagnosed themselves as being unhealthy, they would know I can deal with this level of unhealth if I just do business with this company. And so the checklist is a great way of listing all the pain points a potential customer might have and have them do a self-analysis. I have a friend who runs a clinic down in New Orleans and I recommended he create a healthy body checklist. So it's just things like do you feel tired around two o'clock every day? And then right beneath that would be you might be vitamin B deficient. Did you know that this clinic can give you a vitamin B shot and give you more energy if you are in fact vitamin B deficient. So when you create a checklist, think about the pain points that the potential customer has and every revenue stream being a resolution to one of those pain points. A fourth kind of PDF that you can create is just called a value list. And people love lists. They love three keys to this, five tips on this. I've seen all sorts of great value lists. One gentleman who is a speakers coach, he helps people become speakers created a PDF that just said 52 keys to becoming a great speaker. That's probably overdoing it in terms of the amount of value that they're providing. But anybody who wants to become a speaker is going to download that PDF. You can really take this so many different directions. Ten tips on raising a puppy. Five keys to keeping a green lawn all summer. On and on. Just a list of really valuable bullet points of information that help your potential customer is going to get a great response. You're really going to have a lot of people downloading that. And lastly, fifth, free samples. I say free samples, you can give away physical free samples in a store, but you can also do this with information. If you have a grocery store, for example, you can give away a bunch of great summer recipes. If you have a liquor store, you can give away a bunch of great cocktail recipes for the summer. If you have a hair salon, if you're working for a hair salon, you could give away a style book of new hairstyles that are really popular right now. It's endless, but this idea of giving free samples of what your customer can actually provide, those are terrific lead-generating PDFs. There are an endless number of other examples, but those five get great responses. Let me show you a couple of examples that we've actually seen that have been very productive for our clients. Five mistakes people make with their first million dollars. This was a lead-generating PDF that a financial advisor created. He's going to run an ad in a newspaper for his financial advising firm, but I recommend he don't even run an ad for himself. He run an ad that would cause people to go to a website and download this lead-generating PDF. What did he get in response? He got a ton of email addresses from people who were trying to navigate their first million dollars. What does this mean? It means they're newly wealthy, they don't exactly know what they're doing, and they're looking for his exact skill set, his exact service that he provides. Also an upscale architect created a little PDF, actually it was more like a little e-book called Building Your Dream Home, 10 Things to Get Right Before You Build. Now, who's going to download that? Well, anybody who downloads that, she knows they're looking for an architect and she can call them and follow up. Even if you had one or two people downloading that a week for a big project like Building a Dream Home, she has excellent qualified leads to follow up with. One that I really like is called Cocktail Club, learn to make one new cocktail each month. Say you're a liquor store, you would sign people up for Cocktail Club and they might get a PDF every month, maybe just a one-page sheet that's just a beautifully laid out recipe for making a great cocktail. We've given you some great examples of lead-generating PDFs actually in your workbook. When you print out your workbook, you're going to see a lot of really great examples. We want you to get a feel for how long these things are, how well they're laid out, just so you know exactly what you're trying to write. Just a few odds and ends before we wrap up this particular core discipline. You want a really catchy title. You know, Ray said in a previous module in a previous discipline, you want to scan newspaper headlines, you want to scan magazine headlines, you're looking for what I call clickbait. Have you ever been searching the internet or trying to get work done and you see that title that says, 10 childhood celebrities and what they look like now? You know, oh man, if I click this, it's going to cost me five or 10 minutes, then you click it and you're lost for a minute. You're looking for that kind of title, something that makes people want to read what you've written. Let me just add another note. You know, I've written a lot of books and they're fun to write. They take me about 18 months to write. They're difficult even though they're fun. There's a real meaningful experience about it. I've also written a lot of lead-generating PDFs for my company and for other companies. You know, it's not as fulfilling as writing a book. I'm not going to lie to you, but it's a lot easier and there's a similar experience to it. I encourage you to have a blast. Really put your heart and soul and all your creativity into this. If you ever want to be a book writer someday, this is fantastic practice. It's going to teach you to get to the point very quickly, to create information that's scannable, to make your paragraphs punchy and fun to read. It's terrific practice. So treat every lead-generating PDF as though it's a book and you're a writer and you're John Steinbeck and you're trying to get this right. I think this is going to be one of the most fun practices that you created Disciplinette, creating lead-generating PDFs for your client.
